rawCommits.subject: fix(agents): cascade-delete project bindings when deleting an agent
rawCommits.body:
The server's `delete_agent` returns a 409 ("still added to: Home")
whenever any `project_agent` row references the template, but the UI
never tore down those bindings on the user's behalf — leaving Delete
as a dead-end with no obvious path forward, especially for the
auto-created Home binding that the chat-side self-heal kept
resurrecting.

Centralize the workflow in `useCascadeDeleteAgent`, used by both the
agent detail panel and the sidebar context menu, so confirming Delete
removes every binding first and then deletes the template in one
action. The confirm modal now spells out which projects will be
detached. Server-side, `ensure_agent_home_project_and_binding` skips
the auto-bind when the template is already gone upstream so the
cascade can't race the chat heal.

Also surface why a Home binding may not appear under Home in the
Projects sidebar by hinting "(in another org)" / "(archived)" next to
each row in the agent's Projects tab.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: cascade-delete project bindings when deleting an agent

rawCommits.body:
- Remove global *, *:focus, *:focus-visible { outline: none !important; } from interface/src/index.css that suppressed all focus indicators since Phase 1.
- Add a single :focus-visible base rule (2px outline, --color-focus-ring, 2px offset, --radius-sm) so keyboard focus is visible everywhere; mouse clicks don't trigger focus-visible per modern browser semantics.
- Per-element overrides only on #root and .titlebar-drag, which can receive programmatic focus but are not really keyboard-interactive surfaces; everything else falls through to the global :focus-visible rule. Existing per-component outline:none rules in CSS modules are intentional design choices (e.g. ChatInputBar textarea, sidekick tab bar, AppNavRail taskbar buttons) and are left alone; they all sit at higher specificity or with !important and continue to win.
- Token values (--color-focus-ring: #ffffff dark / #111827 light) come from vendor/zui themes and are not changed in this phase.

rawCommits.subject: feat(theme): default Panel variant to solid so panels match the taskbar bg
rawCommits.body:
ZUI Panel previously defaulted to variant="glass" (rgba(9,9,11,0.15)),
which rendered as a barely-visible overlay against the page bg. The
taskbar pill chrome resolves to color-mix(var(--color-bg) 90%, transparent) -
essentially var(--color-bg). Switching the default variant to "solid"
(which uses var(--color-bg)) makes any unannotated <Panel /> instance
read as the same surface tone as the taskbar pill in dark mode (and
mirror-light in light mode).

Every existing in-tree Panel call site already specifies variant
explicitly (most use variant="solid"), so this default change has no
visible regression risk for current consumers but anchors a more
sensible default for future Panel usage.

rawCommits.subject: feat(settings): merge app settings (Appearance + others) into OrgSettingsPanel
rawCommits.body:
The taskbar gear icon previously opened OrgSettingsPanel ("Team Settings"),
which only contained team-scoped sections (General, Members, Invites,
Rewards, Billing, Z Credit History, Privacy, Integrations). The
appearance/theme controls lived in a separate SettingsView at
/projects/settings/appearance with no UI affordance from the desktop
chrome to reach it. Result: users couldn't find theme controls without
typing the URL.

This consolidates both surfaces into the single modal opened by the
taskbar gear:

- Renames the modal title from "Team Settings" to "Settings" and the
  taskbar gear's title/aria-label from "Team settings" to "Settings".
- Splits the sidebar Navigator into two grouped sections separated by
  small uppercase labels ("APP" / "TEAM"), with App listed first so
  Appearance is immediately visible.
- App-scoped sections (Appearance, Notifications, Keyboard, About,
  Advanced) reuse the existing components from views/SettingsView/* so
  they stay in sync with the desktop deep-link route and the mobile
  settings screen.
- App sections render even when no active org is loaded; the
  "Team settings are currently unavailable" message now only blocks
  team-scoped sections, not the entire modal.
- Section type lives in useOrgSettingsData and is exported so the panel
  can type-narrow against an isOrgSection() guard.

The standalone SettingsView at /projects/settings/:section continues to
exist (still used by the mobile shell and remains valid for deep links).
This change is purely additive on the desktop side.

Tests:
- Adds OrgSettingsPanel cases for app nav items, switching to Appearance,
  the renamed modal title, and Appearance still working when no org.
- Updates the BottomTaskbar test to look for the renamed "Settings"
  button. The pre-existing render-churn test failure (LayoutGrid not in
  its lucide-react mock) is unchanged and out of scope.

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): pin active history key in LRU to stop CEO chat blink
rawCommits.body:
Background sidebar prefetches were evicting the currently-displayed
chat panel's entry from chat-history-store's LRU (capped at
MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES = 8). The CEO/SuperAgent chat is the most common
victim because it sits at index 0 (typically opened first), so the
9th sidebar prefetch dropped it. The renderer then snapped to the
empty state until the next refetch repopulated the entry -- the
"CEO chat blink" failure mode previously masked (but not eliminated)
by the lastNonEmptyRef guard in use-conversation-snapshot.ts. A
re-render burst from the new theme toggle made the race fire often
enough to be reproducible from a single click.

Root-cause fix: add pinnedKeys: Set<string> to chat-history-store
plus pinKey / unpinKey actions, and teach withBoundedHistoryEntry
to skip pinned keys during eviction (with a defensive fallback for
the impossible all-keys-pinned case). useChatHistorySync pins the
active historyKey on mount and unpins on unmount, so the key
backing the currently-rendered chat panel can never be the eviction
victim of a background prefetch. Coverage:
  - 4 new tests in chat-history-store.test.ts (pin survives 10
    prefetches, unpin restores eviction, pinKey idempotent,
    unpinKey no-op for unknown keys)
  - 2 new tests in use-chat-history-sync.test.ts (pin on mount /
    unpin on unmount lifecycle, undefined historyKey skips pin)

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): reveal cold-load transcript proactively to stop CEO black panel
rawCommits.body:
ChatPanel's cold-load reveal relied solely on ChatMessageList's one-shot
`onInitialAnchorReady` callback, which is gated by an `initialLayoutReadyKeyRef`
keyed by streamKey and fires only ONCE per (streamKey, hasMessages=true)
pair. When re-entering CEO/MEOW after the chat-history-store evicted the
events entry (MAX_HISTORY_ENTRIES=8) while the message-store thread for the
same streamKey carried over from the prior visit, the child fired the gate
immediately on first commit (messages.length>0) but `handleInitialAnchorReady`
bailed because historyResolved was still false. When history finally arrived,
the child's gate stayed latched and never re-fired -- leaving
.messageContentHidden (visibility: hidden) permanently applied. The panel
rendered fully black even though sidebar previews kept showing the last
message correctly (previewLastMessages has its own 100-entry bound).

Fix: extend ChatPanel's existing [historyResolved, messages.length] reveal
effect with a third branch that schedules the same double-rAF reveal
directly when cold-load is active with both flags satisfied. The reveal
becomes robust to either ordering of the two signals; the existing
cancelAnimationFrame guard prevents the child callback path and the new
effect path from double-scheduling.

Tests:
- New regression covering the carry-over + late historyResolved race.
- Updated "re-arms cold-load when the user actually switches chats" and
  renamed/flipped "keeps cold-load history hidden until the initial anchor
  is ready"; both were locking in the buggy "transcript stays hidden"
  surface, which the proactive reveal now correctly resolves.

and many referenced an undefined `--color-bg-secondary` token whose
`#1e1e1e` fallback fired in both themes. The result in light mode was
white-on-white text, white-on-white icon hovers, and dark-slab panels
embedded in an otherwise white app shell.

tokens (--color-text-primary, --color-bg, --color-surface,
--color-overlay-*, --color-border) so they flip with the theme.

  theme-aware aliases so the dozens of consumers using these legacy
  names stop falling back to a hardcoded dark hex.
- Bump light-mode --color-text-secondary (#374151) and
  --color-text-muted (#4b5563) to meet WCAG AA contrast on white.

Also invert the AURA wordmark PNG in light mode via a single
[data-theme='light'] img[data-aura-wordmark] { filter: invert(1); }
rule in index.css and tag the three render sites (DesktopTitlebar,
MobileTopbar, LoginView) with data-aura-wordmark. The asset is white
text on transparent, so inversion gives perfect black text without a
second PNG.

moon for dark — even when the stored preference is `system`, so the UI
always tells the user what they're actually looking at.

rawCommits.subject: fix(desktop): make auto-updater self-diagnosing on Windows + macOS
rawCommits.body:
Every prior fix attempt at the "click Install closes the app and
nothing installs" bug was blind: shipped Windows builds use
windows_subsystem = "windows" so all tracing goes to /dev/null, and the
spawned PowerShell handoff only logs from inside its own try block,
hiding tokio panics, signature failures, and silent breakaway-from-job
spawn errors.

Add the diagnostic backbone that future fixes can stand on:

- Layer a tracing-appender daily file at <data_dir>/logs/desktop.log
  so every tracing::warn! survives even with no console.
- New updater::diagnostics module appends every install step
  (install_requested, check_started, download_finished,
  handoff_spawned, ...) to <data_dir>/logs/updater.log and refreshes
  a single-record updater-state.json on every transition. The Windows
  PowerShell handoff appends to the same updater.log so pre- and
  post-exit halves of an install live together.
- New updater::reconcile module reads updater-state.json on boot.
  If the running version matches the persisted target, mark the
  install as a success; if a non-terminal step is older than 30
  minutes, surface a Failed status with the last_step so the user
  sees where the previous attempt died.
- Gate Windows handoff exit on a sentinel file the PowerShell script
  touches as its first action. If PowerShell never starts, do NOT
  exit Aura -- set Failed so the user can retry instead of losing
  the app.
- Replace process::exit(0) on macOS with a graceful
  ShutdownForUpdate handshake + brief drain so tracing flushes and
  sidecars stop cleanly. Verify update.extract_path before relaunch.
- /api/update-status now returns last_persisted_state and a
  diagnostics block with the log paths.
- New POST /api/update-reveal-logs opens <data_dir>/logs in the OS
  file manager. New POST /api/update-stage-only (gated by
  AURA_DESKTOP_DEBUG_UPDATER=1) reproduces the
  download/verify/stage path without exiting Aura.
- UpdateControl renders the failed step ("Stopped at: Handoff
  spawned") and a "Show updater logs" button on both inline and
  panel layouts.

Tests cover the new diagnostics writer, reconcile heuristics, and
sentinel wait paths.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: make auto-updater self-diagnosing on Windows + macOS

rawCommits.subject: feat(chat): add agent MODE selector (Code/Plan/Image/3D) above the input
rawCommits.body:
A new MODE row sits at the top of every agent chat input bar (desktop
ChatInputBar + MobileChatInputBar) with four mutually-exclusive modes:
Code (default), Plan, Image, and 3D. Selection is per-agent and
persisted to localStorage like the model picker, and drives the model
list, the slash-command behavior, and the send pipeline so each mode
steers the system end-to-end.

Modes are modeled as a discriminated union (AgentModeBehavior) and
resolved through ResolvedSend, also a discriminated union, so the
3D variant has no model field, image/chat variants have a non-optional
model: string, and there is no null/undefined drift inside the new
code. Translation back to the legacy onSend wire shape happens once,
at the dispatch boundary.

Image mode injects the generate_image slash command on send
(wire-equivalent to typing /image), 3D injects generate_3d, and Plan
sets action=generate_specs. Picking a mode drops any conflicting chip
so the mode and the chip row never disagree. Slash commands /image and
/3d become a fast keyboard path to setSelectedMode.

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): make mode selector capsule actually animate between segments
rawCommits.body:
The first pass drove the indicator with React state inside a
useLayoutEffect, which committed the new transform in the same
pre-paint tick as the parent's selectedMode change — so the browser
only ever saw the final value and the capsule jumped instead of
sliding. Switch to a "FLIP-style" pattern: render the indicator
unconditionally, never write its style from JSX, and update its
transform/width via a ref inside a post-paint useEffect. The
just-painted DOM still holds the previous transform when the effect
runs, so the next paint becomes a real "previous → new" property
change that the CSS transition animates. The first measurement is
gated by a data-ready attribute so the capsule snaps into place on
mount and only animates on subsequent selection changes.

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): self-heal Home-project binding on bare-agent first chat
rawCommits.body:
A new user's first chat with a freshly created agent was hitting a
422 `Required session identity field aura_session_id is missing for
chat_session` whenever the best-effort auto-bind in
`crud::create_agent::ensure_agent_home_project_and_binding` didn't
take (transient network/storage error, or `agent.org_id` not yet
populated on the network record at create time).

Why it slipped through: commit 2af30d78b ("agents: slash chat setup
latency for CEO-preset turns") moved the bare-agent chat hot path off
`setup_agent_chat_persistence` onto a deduped
`setup_agent_chat_persistence_with_matched` so persistence and
history loading could share a single `find_matching_project_agents`
fetch. The follow-up commit 4d3026a80 ("fix(agents): auto-bind new
agents to a Home project so first chat persists") added the
`lazy_repair_home_project_binding` self-heal — but only inside the
legacy wrapper, which the chat hot path no longer calls. As a result,
when the create-time bind failed silently, the next first-chat turn
returned a structured 422 instead of self-healing the binding the way
`reset_agent_session` still does.

Restore the contract on the hot path:

* Promote `lazy_repair_home_project_binding` to `pub(super)` in
  `chat/setup.rs` so the deduped path can run the same repair without
  re-doing the upstream `list_orgs` / `list_projects_by_org` /
  `list_project_agents` walk twice. The repair already busts the
  per-(jwt, agent_id) discovery cache via
  `invalidate_agent_discovery_cache` and returns the refreshed
  matching list.
* Call it from `agent_route::load_persistence_and_history` when the
  initial `find_matching_project_agents` returns empty. The deduped
  `tokio::join!(persist_fut, history_fut)` then sees the just-created
  binding and `SessionConfig.aura_session_id` is populated, so the
  Tier-1 chat preflight passes.

Add unit tests in `chat::setup::tests` to pin the precondition:
empty matching returns `None` (the orphan state the repair addresses)
and populated matching produces a `ChatPersistCtx` with a real
`session_id` (the post-self-heal happy path that satisfies the chat
preflight).

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

rawCommits.subject: fix(updater): replace fragile PowerShell handoff with cmd.exe .bat
rawCommits.body:
The Windows handoff script was launched via powershell.exe with a 5s
sentinel deadline, but on real machines AMSI / Defender / ExecutionPolicy
introspection of a freshly-written .ps1 routinely takes longer than that
to even reach the script's first line, surfacing as "PowerShell handoff
did not start within 5s" with no further breadcrumbs.

Switch to cmd.exe running a generated .bat - essentially-instant startup,
no AMSI on script load, not gated by ExecutionPolicy / AppLocker - and
make the wait observable instead of opaque:

  * Soft deadline bumped to 30s, hard ceiling 60s. Between them we record
    a HandoffSentinelExtended step rather than failing immediately, so a
    slow AV scan does not abort an otherwise-healthy install.
  * Spawned cmd.exe stdout/stderr are captured to
    <stage>/aura-update-<ver>.bat.{out,err}; on timeout / early-exit the
    tail is spliced into updater.log and a stderr excerpt is included in
    the user-facing error string.
  * Keep the Child handle so try_wait() can detect a child that died
    before writing the sentinel and fail fast with the captured exit code,
    instead of waiting out the deadline for nothing.
  * New UpdateStep variants: HandoffChildExitedEarly,
    HandoffSentinelExtended, HandoffChildOutputCaptured.

Also fixes a pre-existing bug in the breakaway-retry path that passed
log_path.parent() to append_updater_log (which expects data_dir, not the
logs dir, and would have written to <data_dir>/logs/logs/updater.log),
and stops marking that retry as a Failed step since the fallback can
still succeed.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

rawCommits.subject: fix(desktop): share WebContext so File > New Window stays logged in
rawCommits.body:
Secondary main windows used `WebViewBuilder::new()`, giving each window
its own isolated webview storage. The previous workaround baked auth
literals from disk into a per-window initialization script, which left a
new window on `LoginView` whenever the on-disk session was stale,
missing, or rejected by `/api/auth/session`, and prevented login/logout
in one window from propagating to another.

Reuse the primary window's `wry::WebContext` for every secondary main
window via `WebViewBuilder::new_with_web_context(...)`. The shared
context backs localStorage, cookies, and IndexedDB across all main
windows, so a new window inherits the parent's live `aura-jwt` /
`aura-session` directly and login/logout propagates through the same
storage. The secondary init script now only seeds `aura-host-origin` so
a stale disk snapshot cannot clobber the shared in-memory truth.

`LoopState` gains a `web_context: WebContext` field threaded from
`main()` through `BuildLoopStateInput`, so the context outlives the
event loop and is borrowed mutably each time a secondary window spawns.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

rawCommits.subject: fix(evals): unblock smoke job and harden baseline refresh
rawCommits.body:
The Aura Functional Evals workflow has been failing on every push since
Apr 29 (commit 72eac5f4 renamed `desktop-login-host-connection` to
`desktop-login-screen` etc. but never refreshed the baseline). The
smoke job's Playwright run was actually passing; `compare-evals.mjs`
was exiting 1 because three of the four baseline scenarios couldn't be
matched to the candidate summary by id, so each renamed entry was
counted as a missing-scenario regression.

Four changes to break the cycle:

- Refresh `infra/evals/reports/baselines/smoke-summary.json` so its
  scenario ids match `core-browser-smoke.json` again.
- Add `interface/scripts/refresh-evals-baseline.mjs` and an
  `evals:refresh-baseline` npm alias so renaming a scenario id is one
  command (`npm run test:evals:smoke && npm run evals:refresh-baseline
  smoke`) instead of "remember to copy a JSON over".
- Teach `compare-evals.mjs` to detect baseline drift: when at least
  half of the baseline scenarios are missing from the candidate, emit
  a single `[stale-baseline]` banner pointing at the refresh script
  instead of N noisy "missing scenario X" lines that read like real
  regressions. Real regressions (one rogue scenario disappearing) keep
  their per-scenario message.
- Drop the brittle `visibleTexts: ["Login to AURA" / "Sign in"]`
  assertions in the login smoke scenarios in favor of a role-based
  check on the shared "Sign In" submit button. Also skip the
  token / cost / build-step / test-step blocking thresholds when the
  baseline suite is `smoke` (those metrics are always 0 for browser-
  only smoke runs and just add false-positive surface area), and skip
  the duration warning when the baseline duration is 0 so an
  uninitialized baseline doesn't warn on every run.

Documents the new flow in `docs/aura-evals.md`.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

rawCommits.subject: fix(desktop): rename 1024px icon to @2x so cargo-packager can build .icns
rawCommits.body:
cargo-packager 0.11.8's icns generator calls
`icns::IconType::from_pixel_size_and_density(width, height, density)`
where `density` is 2 only when the file stem ends with `@2x`, and the
icns crate's table has no `(1024, 1024, 1)` entry -- ICNS represents
the 1024px slot exclusively as the 512x512 retina (`ic10`) variant at
density=2. So `icon-1024.png` (no suffix -> density=1) hit the `_`
arm and the macOS .app build aborted with `No matching IconType`.

Linux/Windows pass because they emit .ico/AppImage and never enter
the icns path; both macOS legs (arm64 and 15-intel) fail since
ad5a8a611 added `icon-1024.png` to the icons array.

Rename the file to `icon-1024@2x.png` (and update the Cargo.toml
icons list + the `generate-app-icons.mjs` regenerator) so
cargo-packager treats it as the @2x retina slot and writes it to the
.icns as `RGBA32_512x512_2x` (`ic10`). Other sizes are unchanged --
they all map to non-retina ICNS entries at density=1.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: rename 1024px icon to @2x so cargo-packager can build .icns

rawCommits.subject: fix(ci): pre-seed cargo-packager with patched create-dmg for macOS Intel
rawCommits.body:
cargo-packager 0.11.8 hard-codes a download of create-dmg v1.1.1
(commit 28867ba) into ~/Library/Caches/.cargo-packager/DMG/script/create-dmg
and only fetches it when the cache file is missing. v1.1.1 attaches the
DMG without `-nobrowse` and only retries `hdiutil detach` (no force)
3 times, so on the macOS-15 Intel runner Spotlight / fseventsd /
diskimages-helper transiently hold the volume across all 3 attempts and
nightly packaging fails repeatedly with `hdiutil: couldn't eject diskN
- Resource busy`. The workflow-level retry loop just re-runs the same
broken script.

Vendor a patched copy of create-dmg under infra/scripts/release/ that
adds `-nobrowse` on attach, best-effort `mdutil -i off` after mount,
and a `hdiutil detach -force` fallback with 5 retries on busy. Add
install-patched-create-dmg.sh which copies it into the cargo-packager
cache before packaging, and call it both as a new pre-package step and
inside cleanup_retryable_packaging_failure in the three release
workflows so every retry uses our version.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>
rawCommits.cleanSubject: pre-seed cargo-packager with patched create-dmg for macOS Intel

rendered.title: Theming overhaul, desktop chrome, and onboarding land together
rendered.intro: A heavy day for Aura's desktop experience: a multi-phase theming system shipped end-to-end with light mode, custom tokens, and presets; a native-style menu bar and unified titlebar arrived; a first-run onboarding flow went live; and the auto-updater and macOS DMG packaging were rebuilt to be self-diagnosing and reliably shippable.

entries.title: Light mode, theme presets, and a unified Settings surface
entries.summary: A 14-phase theming program landed: design tokens, a custom-token editor with import/export presets, sectioned Settings with deep links, onboarding, and a long tail of light-mode contrast fixes.

items.text: Introduced a token-driven theme system with light/dark variants, a Settings > Appearance section, and Sun/Moon quick toggles in the desktop titlebar and mobile topbar; the toggle was later simplified to a binary light↔dark switch with theme-aware icons.

Shipped a per-token theme editor with named presets, JSON import/export, built-in Aura Dark/Light, and live previews; chrome tokens for sidebar, sidekick, and titlebar are now user-customizable and persisted per resolved theme.

Made light mode actually usable: routed chat bubbles, rails, sidekick, modals, and form inputs through semantic tokens; bumped contrast on muted text; pinned the user message bubble dark in both themes; and added a lint guard against raw color literals in module CSS.

Reorganized Settings into deep-linkable sections (/settings/:section) with About, Appearance, Notifications, Keyboard, and Advanced, and merged app + team settings into a single taskbar-gear modal so theme controls are finally discoverable.

Added a first-run onboarding experience: a 2-step welcome modal, a portal-rendered checklist with 5 tasks, auto-detection via store subscriptions with baseline counts to ignore pre-existing defaults, and a Help button in the taskbar.

Fixed two longstanding chat blink bugs: pinned the active history key in the LRU so background prefetches can't evict the visible chat, and added a proactive cold-load reveal so transcripts no longer render as a black panel after re-entry.

Restored keyboard focus visibility across the app, added a fallback HTML titlebar so window controls remain usable on boot/error overlays, and cascade-deletes for agents now tear down project bindings instead of dead-ending on a 409.
Wired Mixpanel tokens into desktop and mobile nightly builds and defaulted REQUIRE_ZERO_PRO to false on server and desktop so unset envs no longer block sign-in.

entries.title: Agent MODE selector and self-diagnosing auto-updater
entries.summary: Chat input gains a Code/Plan/Image/3D mode switch with persisted per-agent state, and the desktop auto-updater grows a full diagnostic backbone for shipped Windows and macOS builds.

items.text: Added a MODE row above every chat input (desktop and mobile) with mutually-exclusive Code, Plan, Image, and 3D modes that drive model lists, slash-command injection, and the send pipeline end-to-end; selection persists per agent.

Made the desktop auto-updater observable on shipped builds: daily tracing log file, per-step diagnostics with persisted state, boot-time reconcile that surfaces the failed step, sentinel-gated Windows handoff so a stalled PowerShell can't kill Aura, and a graceful macOS shutdown handshake.

Aura3D now remembers context across sessions: the nav restores the last project, switching tabs auto-selects the latest image or model, and project artifacts seed default selections instead of landing on an empty pane.
Extracted a shared InputBarShell from ChatInputBar and Aura3D PromptInput so chat and 3D prompts share one input chrome, and baked the Z_BILLING_API_KEY into desktop builds via build.rs and CI.

entries.title: First-chat self-heal for new bare agents
entries.summary: Restore Home-project binding repair on the deduped chat hot path so a freshly created agent's first message no longer 422s.

items.text: When a new agent's create-time Home-project bind fails silently, the chat hot path now lazily repairs the binding before persistence runs, eliminating the 'aura_session_id is missing for chat_session' error on first chat.

entries.title: Native-style menu bar, desktop right-click, and shared chrome
entries.summary: Aura's desktop chrome got a substantial polish pass: a real menu bar with global shortcuts, a shared right-click menu, login/desktop chrome unified, and the auto-updater's Windows handoff rebuilt on cmd.exe.

items.text: Added File/Edit/View/Help menus to the desktop titlebar with cross-platform shortcut formatting, hover-switching dropdowns, zoom controls, full-screen toggle, and Rust-side IPC for new windows; secondary windows now share the WebContext so login state propagates.

Replaced the Windows updater's PowerShell handoff with a cmd.exe .bat script with captured stdout/stderr, a 30s soft / 60s hard sentinel deadline, and early-exit detection — so AMSI/Defender scans no longer abort otherwise-healthy installs with no breadcrumbs.

Unified login and desktop titlebar chrome via a shared ShellTitlebar pill, then iterated to drop the bespoke wrapper and standardize on zui Topbar with a tokenized BottomTaskbar, and replaced the floating UpdateBanner with a compact Update pill in the titlebar.
Right-clicking empty taskbar chrome now opens the same context menu as the desktop, with edge-aware anchoring and a Settings entry; the menu pre-clamps when launched near viewport edges.

Rebranded installers and the in-app icon to AURA across NSIS, .dmg, .app, and PWA assets via a master-orb icon generator, and pinned iOS TestFlight uploads to Xcode 26 to clear Apple's iOS 26 SDK gate.

Unblocked the long-failing Aura Functional Evals smoke job: refreshed the baseline, added a stale-baseline detector, role-based login assertions, and a one-command refresh script; also tightened the About > Updates row layout at narrow widths.
Hardened macOS DMG packaging by disabling Spotlight on the runner before packaging and force-unmounting busy volumes, recovering CI from the recurring 'hdiutil: Resource busy' wedge.

Polished the chat input mode pill through several iterations — CSS-grid positioning, measured pixel transforms, and inline-style transforms — landing on a reliable sliding capsule animation, plus a leaderboard switch from tokens to estimated cost.

entries.title: Patched create-dmg and Node-24 CI compatibility
entries.summary: Vendored a hardened create-dmg fork to break the macOS Intel packaging deadlock and bumped GitHub Actions runners ahead of the Node 20 deprecation.

items.text: Vendored a patched create-dmg with -nobrowse, mdutil-off, and forced-detach retries, and pre-seeded it into cargo-packager's cache so every nightly retry uses the fixed script instead of the broken upstream v1.1.1.
Renamed the 1024px app icon to icon-1024@2x.png so cargo-packager's icns generator maps it to the retina ic10 slot, fixing the macOS .app build that broke after the AURA rebrand.

Bumped every GitHub Action pinned to Node 20 (checkout, setup-node, setup-java, artifact, cache, setup-python) to its Node-24-compatible major ahead of the June 2026 deprecation, and unblocked Android CI by tightening a leaderboard map type.
Pinned the iOS macos-26 runner to /Applications/Xcode_26.4.app since the runner image ships no plain Xcode_26.app symlink, recovering the validate-ios and ship-ios jobs from xcode-select failures.
Added per-mode accent borders (green for Code, amber for Plan, cyan for Image, purple for 3D) to the segmented mode pill so the active mode reads at a glance.

highlights: Light mode and theme presets ship across desktop and mobile
Native-style File/Edit/View/Help menu bar in the desktop titlebar
First-run onboarding with welcome modal and checklist
Patched macOS DMG packaging unblocks nightly releases
New agent MODE selector (Code/Plan/Image/3D) in the chat input
